hypotheses.claim: Rapid individual maker empowerment via accessible AI tools (as productivity superpowers, ideation accelerators, and career pivots per Andreessen/Malice and All-In discussions) generates structural tension with intelligentsia/taker classes in media, policy, and regulation who benefit from centralized control narratives (safety cartels, expert intermediation, precautionary frameworks); this produces doomer framing and professional groupthink (de Bono rock logic) that seeks to re-intermediate human agency, mirroring Schumpeter's sociological pattern in which dynamic systems subsidize critics whose incentives favor slowing further creative destruction.
hypotheses.notes: Integrates AI video insights on empowerment vs. centralization/doomerism, Schumpeter resentment/intelligentsia dynamics, de Bono operacy vs. entrenched professional consensus, and the media application. Predictive power: Widespread maker adoption of AI will measurably erode zero-sum/doomer politics and increase new venture formation/productivity unless countered by intensified narrative and regulatory capture; observable in longitudinal data on AI usage by non-experts, media framing shifts, and policy outcomes favoring decentralization vs. cartelization.
